title: 烟囱别墅之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\142-烟囱别墅之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
After Monsieur Le Mon departs, Bates informs Anthony that the man found dead near Stanislas has been identified as Gisèppe Manelli, a waiter from the Hotel Britz. Anthony then confronts Balthazar regarding the Stanislas matter, admitting that he was the one who moved the victim's body from the original crime scene to its current location. Anthony explains his motive for coming forward: since it has been proven that the letters in question were not linked to Mrs. Rue, he no longer wishes to withhold information to protect her. Balthazar expresses skepticism about the jury's ability to believe Anthony's story and admits he has been setting "small traps" to catch him.

The two men discuss the murder of Prince Michael. Balthazar believes the murder weapon is hidden within the villa or its courtyard and suggests that searching a bird's nest might yield results. He theorizes that Manelli, a habitual thief, was hired by the Vicomte or the "Red Hand" to steal a memoir. Balthazar suspects that when Manelli instead obtained letters and attempted to blackmail a lady, the Red Hand killed him for his betrayal. Balthazar also suspects the Vicomte's involvement, specifically regarding a gun inscribed "Virginia," which he believes was an attempt to frame Mrs. Rue; however, Anthony reveals he had identified "Virginia" as Michael. Finally, they conclude that the Victorians likely obtained the letters from Manelli on Pound Street, deciphered the code, and will return to the villa to find the diamond.
